title: Rdava-bird

The '''rdava-bird''' was a [[Species/Legends|species]] of brightly [[Color/Legends|colored]] [[Bird/Legends|bird]] native to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Garqi/Legends|Garqi]]. They were small, no larger than the hand of a [[Human/Legends|Human]], and had orange and red plumage. Rdava-birds were commonly found in [[Vlassy Nature Preserve]], where they were admired by visiting tourists. Large numbers of rdava-birds began to [[Death/Legends|die]] off when a leak at an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Chine Gabbard's research station|research station]] spread a pollen-carried [[Nerve toxin/Legends|nerve toxin]] into the nature preserve. Later, a rdava-bird was instrumental in grounding the [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] of [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]], a former [[Corellian Security Force|CorSec]] [[Officer/Legends|officer]] hiding on Garqi.

==Biology and appearance==
The rdava-[[Bird/Legends|bird]] was a [[Species/Legends|species]] of avian.<ref name="Geo">''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]''</ref> They were small and light, with a body slightly smaller than the size of an adult [[Human/Legends|Human]] hand. Atop a plump body, the rdava-bird had a small, rounded head with a round, black [[Eye/Legends|eye]] on each side and a short, pointed, yellow beak. The bird had a straight tail, about the same length as its body. It had two [[Limb/Legends|legs]] and two wings with a wingspan approximately the same length as its body. With these wings, the rdava-birds were capable of flight.<ref name="Union 1" /> Rdava-birds were brilliantly colored,<ref name="Geo" /> with red and orange plumage. The tips of the wings and tail were darker, while the underbelly was lighter-colored.<ref name="Union 1" />

==Behavior==
The rdava-birds grouped into small flocks. Those living within the [[Vlassy Nature Preserve]] were not shy of visitors and were known to alight on tourists' hands or to approach the [[sail ship]]s that traversed the nature preserve. The rdava-birds were active during both daytime<ref name="Union 1" /> and nighttime [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]].<ref name="Missed" />

==History==
The rdava-bird evolved on [[Garqi/Legends|Garqi]], an [[Agriworld/Legends|agriworld]] in the [[Cassander sector/Legends|Cassander sector]]. They were particularly found within the Vlassy Nature Preserve, 800 [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] southwest of Garqi's [[Capital/Legends|capital]], [[Pesktda]]. The nature preserve encompassed the [[Vlassy River]] and was unsuitable for agriculture due to its rugged bluffs and canyons. Birdwatchers from the both the native [[Garqian]] population and offworld tourists visited the Vlassy Preserve to enjoy the brightly plumed birds.<ref name="Geo" />

[[File:Rdava-birds.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Mara Jade with a rdava-bird on her finger.]]
Shortly before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] annexed Garqi and the remainder of the Cassandran Worlds. Under the Empire's hegemony, the [[Botany|botanist]] [[Chine Gabbard]] attempted to transform native [[hafa]] vines into a host capable of carrying [[Nerve toxin/Legends|nerve agents]].<ref name="Geo" />

{{RPGseed}}
Some nerve-toxin-loaded hafa pollen escaped from [[Chine Gabbard's research station|Gabbard's research station]] and was carried downwind to the nature preserve, where it [[Death/Legends|killed]] off large numbers of rdava-birds as well as several biologists who tried to investigate the avians' deaths. Several [[operative]]s that were hired by the scientists managed to break into Gabbard's facility and obtain the antidote before they succumbed to the nerve agent as well.<ref name="Geo" />
{{Endgame}}

In [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]], former [[Corellian Security Force]] operative [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]], who was lying low on the planet after quitting CorSec, was flying his [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighter]] when a rdava-bird was sucked into his starboard engine. The subsequent damage to his [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] grounded the [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] while he stored his X-wing in a [[djorra]]-covered [[Garqi vehicle shed|vehicle shed]] to make repairs.<ref name="Missed" /> Many [[Standard year/Legends|years]] later, in [[20 ABY/Legends|20 ABY]], the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]] traveled to the Vlassy Nature Preserve shortly before their [[Wedding of Luke Skywalker and Mara Jade|marriage ceremony]]. While looking out over the preserve from a high ridge, several rdava-birds flew past them, and one alighted on Jade's finger.<ref name="Union 1" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[Michael A. Stackpole]] created the rdava-birds for his short story "[[Missed Chance]]," originally published in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 7|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 7]] in [[1995]].<ref name="Missed" /> Following this, penciller [[Robert Teranishi]] depicted several unidentified, colorful birds in the Vlassy Nature Preserve on Garqi in the [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] [[Union 1|''Union'' 1]],<ref name="Union 1" /> published on [[November 10]], [[1999]].<ref name="Union1Date">{{WebCite|url=https://gocollect.com/comic/star-wars-union-1|text=Star Wars: Union #1 Value – GoCollect|work=[https://gocollect.com gocollect.com]|archivedate=20230702195621}}</ref> [[Jason Fry]], in composing the Garqi entry in [[2004]]'s ''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]'', connected the rdava-birds with the nature preserve, thus [[Retroactive continuity|retroactively]] identifying the birds in ''Union'' as rdava-birds.<ref name="Fry">{{JCFCite|url=posts/31265966|board=Literature|thread=The Essential Atlas and Galactic Cartography: Official Discussion|user=[[Jason Fry|jasonfry]]|date=2010-09-19|time=7:59 AM|archiveurl=https://archive.is/Ihb8O}}</ref>
